chapters.chapter_summary: I made an incision at the umbilicus and inserted a trocar. I placed the camera down the trocar and inflated the abdomen with carbon dioxide. I made three additional incisions and inserted trocars.
I used surgical instruments to grasp and retract the gallbladder. I identified the cystic duct and artery, and clipped and divided these structures to detach the gallbladder from the liver.
I used an electric cautery device to dissect the gallbladder off the liver. I placed the gallbladder in a bag and inspected the surgical site to ensure no bleeding or leakage.
I inspected the surgical site to confirm that the clips were in good position and that there was no bleeding from the cystic duct, cystic artery, or liver.
I removed the gallbladder through an upper incision and opened it to examine the stones inside. I then closed the surgical site.

SOAP.Subjective: I performed a laparoscopic gallbladder surgery on a patient with symptomatic gallstones. I made an incision at the umbilicus and inserted a trocar to place the camera inside the abdomen.
SOAP.Objective: I identified the cystic duct and artery and clipped and divided these structures to detach the gallbladder from the liver. I used an electric cautery device to dissect the gallbladder off the liver. I placed the gallbladder in a bag and inspected the surgical site to ensure no bleeding or leakage.
SOAP.Assessment: I assessed the procedure as successful with no complications noted. The gallbladder was successfully detached and removed. The clips were in good position, and there was no bleeding from the cystic duct, cystic artery, or liver.
SOAP.Plan: I removed the gallbladder through an upper incision and opened it to examine the stones inside. I then closed the surgical site. Postoperative care will include monitoring for any signs of complications and appropriate pain management.
